review_title: ConvertFlow is the Best Lead Generation Platform Possible
description: I love using ConvertFlow! It's smart tech that makes a big difference like converting organic webinar registrations at 70% vs 25-50% with other tools. That is a huge difference and the company founders and staff care about you and your success which is an unparalleled level of support to grow your business.
author: Mat O'Flynn, D.
pros: ConvertFlow converts the most website visitors out of any of the dozen plus lead generation services I'd used. They make it incredibly easy to convert anonymous website visitors into customers and dynamically update calls to actions and content on your site based on segments you can establish like first-time website visitors, repeat customers or any other variable. ConvertFlow is my favorite online service without question.
cons: ConvertFlow already replaces form services like TypeForm, Gravity Forms, Wufoo and all others but it would be a great to be able to upload attachments on CTAs and potentially integrate Stripe or another payment processor for simple paywall content using ConvertFlow's great landing page builder.

review_title: Holy Grail of lead form softwares.
description: So far we've gone through a big exercise of switching our old forms over and having done this before I have to say – Convertflow is the easiest-to-implement form builder I've ever used. I had exactly zero bugs or problems getting it to talk to my CRM and website. My team easily picked it up and we made 30+ changes in just a few days.
author: Jacob S.
pros: This is the 1 single form system I've found that does everything. Pages, exit pop-ups based on deep levels of logic, inline form embeds, sidebar/sticky bar, multi-step forms and logic-based questions, AND connects to our CRM flawlessly AND doesn't glitch regularly. For 5 years I've been using 3-4 systems to do all this and using zapier to connect them. I've tried Leadformly, leadpages, convertful, typeform, ninja forms... probably more I'm forgetting
cons: I wish the touchpoint/source data was available on the $99/month plan not just the $199 plan but it's hard for me to argue with the value
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great idea but critical pieces missing
description: So in the end I think ConvertFlow holds a lot of promise, but may not stay in business long enough to achieve the high goals it has set out for itself. I hope that it does. I hope that they do what is needed in the area of support and bug fixes, but I have used it for a long time and seen virtually no change to the bug and a massive drop in the quality of support.
author: Kevin P.
pros: Has all the basic components for an amazing lead collection tool Offers various survey, forms, landing pages and embedding options It tries to provide a lot of flexibility in creation and deployment.
cons: The creation of these surveys, forms and landing pages is riddled with bugs, and the bugs have persisted without fix for the full time I have used the software, which is probably just over a year. It is not unusual to have their server fail and cause additional delays and problems. What has happened more recently is much more troubling. Their customer support has ground to a complete stand still. Problems that used to take minutes to resolve have now stretched into days. For me this is a sure sign of the beginning of the end unless they can move a number of developers into support and keep their existing base of customers functional.

review_title: Very straightforward and easy to implement
author: Tung N.
pros: It has all the features necessary to support lead scoring, lead nurturing and email marketing. Combining ConvertFlow with ActiveCampaign allows for frictionless visitor segmentation so we can deliver the right message to the right people.
cons: It is rather costly for small businesses to get started with ConvertFlow, since the cheapest tier costs $29/month. Although the functionalities offered by ConvertFlow cover all the basic marketer needs, small businesses may be hesitant to get started with ConvertFlow without fully understanding what the tool has to offer.

review_title: Helpful for forms, pop-ups, and sticky bars
author: Alex P.
pros: ConvertFlow has a range of options available to use. We've used their landing pages to create stand-alone pages that don't necessarily make sense to build within WordPress. We use their sticky bars to advertise sales or special announcements. And we've used their hooks and overlays to offer free downloads. I like the range of options available and find them easy to implement.
cons: I integrate ConvertFlow data to Salesforce using Zapier, which works, but I wish it had built-in integration with Salesforce. And while I've generally mastered the set-up/design process now, the way that you program the layout is a bit counter-intuitive. Luckily they have a helpful support team who has been able to help answer my questions whenever I have them.

review_title: Convert Flow is the glue for my entire marketing platform
description: I recommend ConvertFlow to just about all my clients.
author: Eric H.
pros: Marketing is hard work. It's takes so much effort to get a lead, and once they've arrived at my website and they respond to my CTA, signing up for a newsletter, or downloading an eBook, that click represents the culmination of considerable effort. ConvertFlow powers that click, easily moving the information into my email marketing system and CRM. But what I love most is the ability to re-target my CTAs, inline in my website's content, with appropriate follow up CTAs. ConvertFlow is the glue that holds together my entire marketing platform.
cons: Whatever limitations I've ever discovered with ConvertFlow have been quickly addressed by their team in chat. And if there really is a limitation, I often see it addressed in new versions. Their team is highly responsive to user feedback.

review_title: Great features at a reasonable price
description: We've been using ConvertFLow for our lead generation for over 2 years and I've been really happy with the software. I don't have any plans on switching anytime soon.
author: Cory B.
pros: ConvertFlow is super easy to use and implement. I really like the integration with Infusionsoft which allows us to segment and tag customers appropriately.
cons: The only downside I've run into is having a bit of problem with the logic that prevents popups from showing to certain users. The workflow around this could be improved a little.

review_title: A good automation tool for businesses
description: The software is a really nice one, they know exactly what they're doing, the interface isn't confusing and it's easy to use.Even if I couldn't use it to it's full potential because I couldn't afford the paid plan, I really love the software.If it is something you could afford then I would definitely recommend it to you.
author: Favour N.
pros: I like how it is easy to use and integrate.I also like the quality of the software, it look very professional and organized.I really love the insights and analytics feature, it helps businesses know how well their business is doing.I also love the lead capturing, email and marketing automation features, I love how it helps businesses market and grow their email list while also growing thier business on automation.
cons: I don't like how very limited the free plan is, there are lots of things that isn't available in the free plan.I also don't like how expensive it is, if you're looking for a cheap automation software for your business then this isn't for you, a startup business won't be able to afford it, the software was designed with top businesses in mind, I wish they could reduce the price to fit everyone's pocket.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Great software for capturing leads
description: I've really enjoyed using ConvertFlow. It is a great lead capture software and you can quickly spin up many lead capture tools and A/B test them. I definitely recommend you try it out.
author: Chris J.
pros: I really like ConvertFlow's drag and drop builder. It allows someone with little design experience quickly build landing pages, popups and many other lead capture tools. It also allows a lot of customizability because you can add code snippets to the header and footer. This allows you to insert script tags (like a facebook pixel tag). The other thing I love about ConvertFlow is being able to A/B test. It is an amazing tool for that and you can set up tests in minutes. The reporting on your A/B tests is fantastic as well. It does hookup with many other tools (like email automation) so you can have your leads connect to your other systems. It also integrates with Google Analytics and it will automatically create events there.
cons: I've found two things I don't like about ConvertFlow. I can't export reports. This is very frustrating because it makes it hard to run reports for anyone who doesn't have access to ConvertFlow. The other issue is the Google Analytics integration doesn't work for Landing Pages. If I could do that then the reporting issue above would be fine.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Convert It Does
description: I needed a quality solution that was easy to integrate and execute. After a thorough search, ConvertFlow was came through and their customer service has been top notch. They want my business.
author: Letha D.
pros: This software has the most value for the budget, the options are endless, but simple to integrate and implement. Tons of templates for any type of need. Customer service is quick and responsive.
cons: I don't have anything I don't like thus far, it's only worked well for me.

review_title: Smart On-Site Communication and Conversion Tools That Help You Grow Your Business
description: I was impressed with how much you can do with ConvertFlow. It's more advanced than other tools on the market, but you can use the basic features within minutes if you want to. There's no need to get lost in advanced settings when you don't need them at the moment. It is however great to know you can take things to the next level when you need it, without having to change to other tools. This is a smooth, modern tool that can be very powerful for your user engagement and conversions in your business. Your site becomes a whole lot smarter if you use this and this will impact your results (=conversions).
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Well, where should I start? This is an advanced tool or should I say suite of tools. You've got the standard messages and pop-ups and other widgets that you can show to your visitors, but you can also go pretty advanced with this system, like showing certain things to certain site visitors, based on their profile and history (returning visitor versus new visitor, email subscriber version no email subscriber, etc.). I like how smart you can make your website, by adding some piece of code to your pages. This way you can really communicate on a different, personal level with each visitor. The interface is very modern and intuitive, making this a tool that doesn't have a big learning curve. That's what I love the most about ConvertFlow.
cons: There's a lot to discover in this tool. I've used a couple of features and noticed there are more advanced settings that I didn't touch yet. I don't have any issues with this tool and will definitely use the more advanced features when I feel I need them later down the road.

review_title: You won't regret ConvertFlow a a small biz owner!
description: ConvertFlow has made it very easy for me to create a sticky website and to take advantage of overlays and embedded forms where my audience can opt in to some of my services and freebies. I've been able to really get granular on my lead capture and further monetize my business and investments. I love seeing the data roll in and using it as a tool to continually optimize my CTAs based on the traffic they're getting (or not getting!).
author: Nicole C.
pros: After trying and failing to create my own customized popup and embedded banners through various plugins available via my website (Wordpress), I finally found ConvertFlow and it changed my life. I love how easy and intuitive their platform is and how customizable their banners, pop ups and landing pages are. I love designing stuff, but I am definitely not a coder so I love that I can just copy and paste the HTML directly into my website and link it on the backend. They have a variety of templates to choose from and I feel that for the price, I definitely get my money's worth. As a small business owner you have to balance your time investment with outsourcing costs and for me, investing in ConvertFlow was worth the money and time saved. I have so much fun designing new pop up banners and embedded forms. And they also integrate with other services I use, such as MailChimp, which makes lead capture a breeze!
cons: As of right now, ConvertFlow does everything I need it do. I could probably spend more time configuring tags on the backend for data analysis.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Bets lead capturing tool
description: We're primarily using Convertflow to promote webinars and seasonal offers (e.g. Black Friday), and integrate it with our CRM platform, which in turn allows us to accurately nurture the captured leads.
author: Berk D.
pros: The flexibility it provides when it comes to creating a pop-up (CTA) is nothing short of amazing. There are 20+ layouts to choose from, it's possible to save user profiles, can switch between light/dark themes, full screen or not, etc. Their chat support is very responsive, never had to wait more than a minute to get a problem solved.
cons: The user interface is a bit clunky and can get unresponsive at times. They're pushing out "Convertflow 2.0" as I'm writing this, but I'm yet to test it myself. I would assume they fixed the said clunkiness though.

review_title: Purpose of use great, ease of use needs improvement
description: Having ConvertFlow has helped our business overall, and I am glad we are using it! It is frustrating at times in the process of building a campaign, but overall we are better off with it than without it.
author: Brady W.
pros: I love that you can A/B test easily and see the results quickly from it. This service has helped us more easilycommunicate discounts and new features to our customers. Easy to implement on website once built.
cons: “Drag and drop” for building campaigns is not as easy as it sounds. Frequently would have to publish a Campaign not exactly how I wanted it because the font wouldn’t change correctly, or some other technological issue. When I contacted customer service, it look about 8 mins to get an answer on chat and did not get a resolution.

review_title: The spine of my lead gen.
author: Geoffrey H.
pros: - Easy to use and setup - Many templates available for any kinds of CTAs. Works great to generate Landing Pages as well - Well integrated with Segment as well as Zapier - Good for personalization
cons: - Gets a bit slow when your library has many CTAs or when trying to edit a heavy landing page - Navigating between edition and preview is not always easy - Impossible to save parts of CTAs and landing pages to add to a new one

review_title: ConvertFlow is best in class
author: Matthew C.
pros: Lots of functionality / features Great support Easy to use Fast and responsible
cons: There's not much to dislike. It really is exceptional.

review_title: Convertflow was a great choice
description: Overall it's been a great experience with Convertflow. I found this service when searching for sticky bars that were compatible with Intercom. During my free trial, I started using the pop-ups and other products as well.
author: Jonathan W.
pros: I liked the fact that it integrated well into our website and is compatible with Intercom.
cons: I have to pay so much extra to add to additional websites.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Landing Page CTA's bring revenue
description: Once I build my first CTA it was easy to launch and monitor my campaign, and their support is very good. Also they have very good tutorials
author: Josué M.
pros: Compared with other platforms, it's easy and cheap to get lead generation insights, attribution and conversion comparison.
cons: CSS design require coding. Once I duplicate a landing page I need to change my coding to re-asign css properties to cloned sections and elements.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Strong features at a good price
description: ConvertFlow has a lot of muscle, while remaining light and fairly easy to work with. Support are great, and clear, when you do get stuck.
author: Carolyn W.
pros: The multi-step forms are priceless and a friendly way to convert. We get more leads with them than with normal static forms. The personalisation is targeting is well works really well, too. When designing elements, consistent brand is easy with the customisable colour palette.
cons: Sometimes, when working with the forms, you need to delete a field you want to change and build a new field, rather than being able to edit it. But the developers seem to have started removing this issue.

review_title: Helped us create an effective funnel
description: It's been great. The way we've used the tool is to automate a pop-up on our site that asks people if they have questions. This works very well to help people who are looking for something specific. Those questions are emailed to our [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] who responds promptly. We then add them to our email list.
author: Cody B.
pros: Ease of use. Capability of automation. UX/UI
cons: It's a little pricey, but it's not not worth the value.

review_title: A cost-effective marketing tool for setting up campaigns, A/B testing, landing pages in minutes
author: Karen C.
pros: Convertflow is incredibly easy to use. The best feature is all the beautiful pre-built templates (pop-ups, forms, and landing pages) that make it easy to customize to our liking. Convertflow also integrated with our CRM.
cons: Some features that I would like to see is adding more drag-and-drop elements beyond forms, buttons, text boxes, and so son. While adding custom CSS and HTML is an option, added elements would help out the non-tech-savvy marketers.

review_title: Advanced Lead Capturing for Solo Entrepreneurs
author: Tim H.
pros: The deep integration with my email software ActiceCampaign
cons: Sometimes hard-to-understand targeting rules, as well as the performance of the application itself.

review_title: Great potential, but needs to develop
author: Nick S.
pros: The integration process is quite easy and designing the properties straight forward.
cons: It is missing a way to identify customers via url parameters os you can integrate seamlessly with mail campaigns.

review_title: Great tool for lead generation
author: Jeroen V.
pros: Easy to use and a lot of templates. Good customer service and product development.
cons: Maybe the UX of the funnels/cta's. Difficult to understand at first. You need have some knowledge to understand all the options.

review_title: Amazing product!
description: ConvertFlow has well-exceeded my expectations! I am very happy that I discovered ConvertFlow!
author: Brad S.
pros: Having ConvertFlow on my site feels like having secret super powers. I can segment audiences, use text merge fields to personalize pages with the user's name, I can show an offer to a user who hasn't seen it and hide it if the user has. I have ConvertFlow integrated with MailChimp so that if a visitor is already subscribed to my mail list, I don't keep pestering them to sign up. I use ConvertFlow to offer downloads in exchange for a user's email sign-up, but if they're already subscribed, I just take them straight to the download page.
cons: Absolutely nothing! I love this product!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
